name: Lanikai Beach
tagline: Consistently ranked among the world's most beautiful — turquoise water so clear it looks fake.
parking: No public lot — park at Kailua Beach Park and walk 10 min
hours: Open 24 hours (public beach access via neighborhood paths)
bestTime: Early morning for calm water and fewer people; sunrise is spectacular
talkStory: Lanikai means 'heavenly sea' in Hawaiian — a name coined in the 1920s when the area was first developed as a residential neighborhood. There is no public parking lot, no restrooms, and no lifeguards by design; residents fought to keep it that way. Access is through narrow blue-signed paths between beachfront homes on Mokulua Drive that are easy to miss. Do the Lanikai Pillbox hike at sunrise first — the aerial view of the Mokulua Islands from the WWII bunkers above is one of Oahu's most iconic sights, then cool off with a swim below.
